title: Intense Crunchy Filipino White Cornmeal Menudo
description: A weeknight Filipino dish featuring white cornmeal and long-fermentation-dough, with a bitter and crunchy texture, made with ayurvedic-rajasic-friendly ingredients. This menudo is a unique and flavorful twist on traditional Filipino cuisine.
instructions: In a large bowl, combine the white cornmeal and long-fermentation-dough, mixing well to incorporate.
Heat the vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium heat, then add the chopped onion and cook until translucent.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional 1-2 minutes, until fragrant.
Add the diced tomato and cook for 2-3 minutes, until the mixture is soft and well combined.
Add the fish sauce, if using, and stir to combine.
Add the cornmeal and dough mixture to the skillet, stirring to combine with the tomato mixture.
Cook for an additional 5-7 minutes, until the mixture is crispy and golden brown, stirring frequently.
Season with salt, black pepper, and turmeric, to taste.
Serve hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs, if desired.
